title: Using Oracle for the Repository
text: Using Oracle for the Repository Oracle can be used for the Uniface Repository. Consider the following when storing the Uniface Repository in Oracle: The Uniface meta dictionary uses variable-length storage techniques with platform-specific data formats. To access the dictionary via SQL*Net, you must install Uniface with the independent byte order option set. You can store the Repository in Oracle only when the default packing code mapping of the connector is used. However, you can set the following connector options before creating the Repository: disable segmented fields can be set because there are no segmented fields in the dictionary. For more information, see disable segmented fields . map fixed length to variable can be set. For more information, see map fixed length to variable . Uniface assigns a special meaning to some characters in VARCHAR2, CHAR and LONG fields in the Repository. Uniface does not work correctly if these characters are corrupted when Oracle converts character data between character sets. To avoid this problem, define the Oracle client character set . You can do this by using the Oracle NLS_LANG environment variable. To improve performance, it is recommended that (if your system setup can support it) you allow the ORA connector to open between 150 and 250 cursors when accessing the Repository in Oracle. The number you need depends on how extensively you use the features of the Uniface IDE. For more information, see open cursors . When you test forms in Uniface, you may want to allow extra cursors for the tables accessed by those forms. This is only necessary if you access the test data on the same logon path as the Repository. The connector option disable cache lowers the number of cursors the Uniface IDE keeps open (cursor attached to cached statement) in the application to a minimum. However, setting this will decrease the performance. For more information, see disable cache . You can use the $E6_BASE_DATE assignment setting to store 0-1-0000 for empty Time and DateTime fields. The format is as follows: [SETTINGS] $E6_BASE_DATE 0000 Copy For more information, see $E6_BASE_DATE Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
